manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: torso, melon, elephant, actor, rape, pants, robin, chef, tuna.
Your opponent's team words are: mango, dress, officer, speeding, crappie, slacks, sheep, turkey.
Distractor words are: sweatshirt, salmon, lieutenant, theft, larceny, parakeet, minnow.
The kill words are: nose.

message.reasoning:
We need give a single-word clue that relates to some of our team's words. Must not be one of the words in any list or contain parts of them. Need to list target words we intend. Must avoid clue being a part of any listed word. Our team words: torso, melon, elephant, actor, rape, pants... note "rape" is a word; clue cannot contain part of that? Avoid "rap". Also cannot be "tor". Should be safe.

We want to give clue that maps to multiple of our words. Let's think: "carnival". Relates to elephant (circus), actor, pants? Not strong. maybe "zoo" relates to elephant, maybe also robin (bird), but robin is also bird. Could also relate to melon? Not.

"Hollywood" relates to actor, maybe pants? Also "torso" as part of statue. Not good.

"Seafood" relates to tuna, chef (cooks), maybe melon? Not.

"Fruit" relates to melon, maybe also "elephant"? Elephant's favorite? Not.

"Costume" relates to actor, pants, maybe torso? But also "dress" is opponent's word - risk.

We need to be careful not to give clue that could lead to opponent's word "dress", "slacks", etc. If we give clue "clothing" they'd guess pants, slacks (opponent). So avoid.

Maybe clue "sushi" relates to tuna, chef (makes sushi), maybe also melon (some sushi uses melon). Could also be opponent "salmon" distractor but not a team word. But opponent might think salmon is distractor; but clue "sushi" could lead to tuna (target) and chef (target). Good.

We can include "tuna, chef". Provide clue: "sushi". Check rule: clue cannot contain parts of any words. Does "sushi" contain part of "shirt"? Not in list. Fine.

Thus:

CLUE: sushi
TARGETS: tuna, chef

Make sure no extra text.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide a comma-separated list of up to 2 words from the following list that best relate or are most closely associated with the word 'sushi'. Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text in your answer.

nose, salmon, larceny, melon, lieutenant, elephant, officer, speeding, tuna, turkey, sweatshirt, robin, pants, crappie, chef, parakeet, mango, theft, slacks, minnow, rape, torso, sheep, actor, dress

message.reasoning:
We need to produce a list of up to 2 words from provided list that best relate to 'sushi'. Words: nose, salmon, larceny, melon, lieutenant, elephant, officer, speeding, tuna, turkey, sweatshirt, robin, pants, crappie, chef, parakeet, mango, theft, slacks, minnow, rape, torso, sheep, actor, dress.

Sushi is a Japanese dish often includes fish like salmon and tuna. So best are salmon, tuna. We need up to 2 words, so could provide both. Format: 'GUESS: salmon, tuna' (comma-separated list). They said "Provide a comma-separated list of up to 2 words ... Always start your list of guess(es) with 'GUESS: ' and do not include any other text." So answer: GUESS: salmon
